entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced 2008 with improved injector seals and emissions compliance for JK Wranglers.
commonFailures: Injector seal "Black Death" failure causing soot buildup and catastrophic damage
Water pump plastic impeller degradation leading to overheating
Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage units

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: Early 2.8 CRD code found in pre-2008 JK models, similar architecture to ENS.
commonFailures: Injector seal failure identical to ENS units
Early timing chain tensioner design prone to stretch
EGR valve clogging on short-journey vehicles
entries.cta: Get quotes for EKG Jeep Wrangler engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: Later 2.8 CRD variant with refined emissions hardware for post-2014 models.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues on urban-driven vehicles
Turbo actuator sticking causing reduced power
Injector seal wear still present despite refinements
entries.cta: Get quotes for VM11D Jeep Wrangler engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol

compatibleVariants: Sport, Sahara, Rubicon, Unlimited, Willys, 80th Anniversary

Cylinder head cracking on 2011-2013 casting batches
Oil cooler housing leaks dumping oil into engine valley

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Updated Pentastar variant with eTorque mild-hybrid system for JL models.
commonFailures: Same rocker arm/camshaft vulnerability as ERB
eTorque belt tensioner wear on hybrid components
Cylinder head issues resolved post-2014 but monitor early JL units

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol
entries.history: Transitional V6 for early JK models, less powerful than Pentastar but simpler.
commonFailures: High oil consumption from piston ring wear
Exhaust manifold bolt failure causing leaks
Lower power output strains engine under load
entries.cta: Get quotes for EGT Jeep Wrangler engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Legendary straight-six renowned for durability, fitted to iconic TJ Wranglers.
commonFailures: Cylinder head crack on 0331 casting (1999-2001 units)
Exhaust manifold bolt shear from corrosion
Oil pump drive gear wear on high-mileage examples

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Wrangler Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Wrangler's most prominent engines - the 2.8 CRD diesel and 3.6 Pentastar petrol - dominate UK replacement enquiries due to well-documented failure patterns. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Wrangler.

problems.h4: 3.6 Pentastar Rocker Arm & Camshaft Failure - Why Early JK/JL Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Wrangler JK/JL Sport, Sahara, Rubicon, Unlimited (2011-2018, ERB/ERC Pentastar)
problems.rootCause: Factory rocker arms wear prematurely, allowing valve lash to increase and damage the camshaft lobes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 24 rockers and inspect camshaft before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early via ticking noise; prevents catastrophic damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace damaged rockers, camshaft, lifters; machine head if scored
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function but doesn't address underlying design weakness

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded rockers, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Wranglers worth £15k+; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: If you hear a persistent ticking from the top of your Pentastar, act immediately - a £950 preventative rocker replacement beats a £4,500 engine replacement. On a Wrangler valued over £12,000, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is often the smarter long-term investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt ERB/ERC Pentastar engine prices for your Jeep Wrangler

problems.h4: 2.8 CRD Injector Seal "Black Death" Failure - Diesel Wrangler Catastrophe
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Wrangler JK 2.8 CRD Sahara, Sport, Unlimited, Rubicon (2007-2018, ENS/EKG/VM11D)
problems.rootCause: Copper injector seals degrade, allowing combustion gases to blow past and carbonize oil into abrasive soot.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace copper seals and washers, clean carbon buildup
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before soot damages cylinder head or piston

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once soot contamination is visible; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: Black soot around injectors means the damage is already done - don't waste money on seal replacement alone. For a JK Wrangler worth £10k+, a rebuilt 2.8 CRD with upgraded seals offers far better reliability and resale value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for ENS/EKG 2.8 CRD engine replacement for your Jeep Wrangler

problems.h4: Pentastar Cylinder Head Crack (2011-2013) - Casting Defect Recall
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Wrangler JK 3.6 Pentastar (2011-2013, ERB early casting)
problems.rootCause: Early cylinder head casting had weak spots between valves, allowing coolant/oil mixing under thermal stress.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, machine or replace cracked head, reseal, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; verify casting date before proceeding

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated head casting, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if block shows signs of coolant contamination or warping

problems.recommendation: Check your VIN for the extended warranty campaign first - some 2011-2013 Wranglers qualify for free head replacement. If not covered, a rebuilt engine with the updated casting eliminates the defect permanently.
problems.cta: Check if your 2011-2013 Wrangler qualifies for Pentastar head warranty

problems.group: 4.0 AMC Straight-Six Cylinder Head Crack (0331 Casting)
problems.h4: 4.0 AMC Straight-Six Cylinder Head Crack (0331 Casting) - TJ Era Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Wrangler TJ 4.0 (1999-2001, AMC 242 with 0331 head casting)
problems.rootCause: 0331 casting had thin metal between exhaust valves 3 & 4, prone to cracking under thermal stress.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cracked head with another 0331 casting (still at risk)
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; same casting defect remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace with improved 0630 casting head (post-2001 design)
Supply & fit rebuilt engine with 0630 head, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-value TJs or Rubicons; ensures decades more service

problems.recommendation: If your TJ has the 0331 head and shows overheating symptoms, upgrade to a rebuilt engine with the 0630 casting - it's the only permanent fix for this legendary engine's one flaw.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AMC 242 4.0 engine prices for your Jeep Wrangler TJ

problems.h4: 3.8 EGT Oil Consumption & Sludge Buildup - Early JK Petrol Weakness
problems.rootCause: Piston ring design allowed excessive oil consumption; neglected changes led to sludge and bearing wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, flush sludge, use high-mileage oil
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary mitigation; doesn't fix root ring design issue

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated rings, tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for Wranglers worth £8k+; resolves consumption permanently

problems.recommendation: If your 3.8 burns over 1L per 1,000 miles, repair is rarely cost-effective. A rebuilt unit with improved rings offers better reliability and protects your Wrangler's resale value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EGT 3.8 engine replacement for your Jeep Wrangler JK

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Wrangler
finalCta.paragraph: Wranglers hold their value exceptionally well - a £3,000 engine replacement can extend the life of a £15,000 JK or JL by another 100,000 miles. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Jeep Wrangler specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components to address known failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the trail without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Wrangler Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Wrangler and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Jeep Wrangler Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Jeep Wrangler in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Jeep Wrangler 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8L CRD diesel powered most UK JK Wranglers from 2007-2018, offering strong torque for off-roading and towing, with ENS and EKG codes dominating replacement enquiries due to injector seal and water pump failures.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Wrangler 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Wrangler 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L EcoDiesel V6 arrived in the JL generation (2020-present), offering improved refinement and fuel economy over the 2.8 CRD, but with less UK market penetration and higher replacement costs due to specialist availability.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch on early units
DPF regeneration issues on urban use
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Wrangler 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Wrangler 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L turbo "Hurricane" four-cylinder debuted in the JL generation (2018-present), offering Pentastar-like power with better fuel economy and stop-start technology, though long-term reliability data is still emerging.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Wrangler 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.6L Pentastar V6 replaced the 3.8 in 2011 and remains the dominant petrol engine for JK/JL Wranglers, prized for power but notorious for rocker arm and cylinder head failures on early units.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler 3.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Wrangler 3.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.8L V6 powered early JK Wranglers (2007-2011) before the Pentastar arrived, offering adequate power but higher oil consumption and lower refinement than its successor.
commonFailurePoints: Excessive oil consumption from piston ring wear
lower power straining engine under load
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler 3.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Wrangler 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary 4.0L AMC straight-six powered the TJ generation (1997-2006), renowned for durability but with a known cylinder head casting weakness on 1999-2001 units.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Wrangler 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5L AMC four-cylinder was the base engine for early TJ Wranglers (1997-2002), offering modest power and simplicity but less torque for off-road use.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on overheated units
valve guide wear on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Wrangler 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.2L AMC straight-six powered early YJ Wranglers (1987-1990), featuring carburetion on base models and fuel injection on later units, with classic simplicity but lower output.
commonFailurePoints: Carburetor tuning issues on early models
lower power requiring careful gearing for off-road use
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler 4.2L petrol engine prices

families: ERB/ERC - 3.6 Pentastar V6 (2011+), rocker arm/camshaft failure risk
EGT - 3.8 V6 (2007-2011), high oil consumption and lower power
AMC 242 - 4.0 straight-six (1997-2006), 0331 head casting crack on early units
knownFor: Pentastar delivers strong power but requires vigilance for rocker arm ticking
4.0 AMC straight-six is famously durable except for 0331 casting head cracks
3.8 EGT suffers oil consumption that can lead to sludge if neglected
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler Petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Wrangler Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: Mild-hybrid eTorque technology arrived on JL Pentastar engines (2020+), offering efficiency gains without plug-in complexity.
items.descriptor: Mild-hybrid eTorque technology arrived on JL Pentastar engines (2020+), offering efficiency gains without plug-in complexity.
families: 48-volt starter-generator provides torque assist and enables stop-start
Recovers braking energy to recharge small lithium-ion battery
No plug-in capability; engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection
importantNotes: eTorque components add complexity but improve fuel economy by ~2-3 mpg
Rebuilt engines must include hybrid system compatibility verification
Availability more limited than standard Pentastar units

engineYears.h2: Jeep Wrangler Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Jeep Wrangler's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The YJ brought the Wrangler nameplate to Europe, with carbureted and fuel-injected AMC engines.
keyChanges: First Wrangler sold in UK, replacing CJ series
AMC 2.5L four-cylinder and 4.2L straight-six offered
Carburetion on base models, throttle-body injection on later units
knownFor: Simple, rugged design ideal for modification
Carburetor tuning challenges on early models
AMC engines durable but lower power by modern standards
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1995 Jeep Wrangler replacement engines

years.description: The TJ revived classic styling with coil-spring suspension and the legendary 4.0 straight-six.
keyChanges: Coil-spring suspension improved ride and articulation
AMC 4.0L straight-six became the dominant engine
Rubicon trim introduced (2003) with locking differentials
knownFor: 4.0 AMC 242 renowned for durability and simplicity
Cylinder head crack risk on 0331 casting (1999-2001)
High enthusiast demand keeps values strong
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997-2006 Jeep Wrangler replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Wrangler TJ 4.0 engine replacement • 0331 cylinder head crack fix • AMC 242 rebuilt engine cost

years.preview: JK Generation Launch: New Chassis, 3.8L V6
years.description: The JK brought a wider track, improved safety, and the transitional 3.8L V6 alongside the new 2.8 CRD diesel for UK buyers.
keyChanges: All-new chassis with improved crash protection
3.8L EGT V6 replaced 4.0L for petrol models
2.8L CRD diesel (EKG/ENS) introduced for UK/Europe
knownFor: 3.8L EGT suffers oil consumption and lower power vs 4.0
2.8 CRD offers strong torque but injector seal "Black Death" risk
Wider stance improves stability but reduces articulation vs TJ
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2010 Jeep Wrangler replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Wrangler JK 3.8 engine replacement • 2.8 CRD injector seal failure • JK diesel engine cost UK

years.description: The 3.6L Pentastar V6 replaced the 3.8L, bringing more power but also early cylinder head casting defects.
keyChanges: 3.6L ERB Pentastar V6 introduced for petrol JK models
Cylinder head recall issued for 2011-2013 Pentastar casting defect
2.8 CRD refined with ENS code for improved emissions
knownFor: Pentastar delivers 285hp but rocker arm/camshaft failure risk emerges
Cylinder head crack on early ERB castings (2011-2013)
Extended warranty campaign covers some affected VINs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2013 Jeep Wrangler replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Wrangler Pentastar cylinder head crack • 3.6 rocker arm failure cost • 2012 Wrangler engine recall
enquiries: Jeep Wrangler Pentastar cylinder head crack

years.description: Post-recall Pentastar engines and refined 2.8 CRD units made late JK models more reliable, though rocker arm vigilance remains essential.
keyChanges: Updated Pentastar casting eliminates cylinder head crack risk
2.8 CRD receives VM11D code for improved emissions hardware
Final JK production year (2018) before JL launch
knownFor: Late JK Pentastar units more reliable but still monitor for rocker ticking
2.8 CRD injector seal failures remain the dominant diesel failure mode
Strong residual values make engine replacement financially sensible
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2018 Jeep Wrangler replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Wrangler JK final year engine • Pentastar rocker arm replacement • 2.8 CRD rebuilt engine UK
enquiries: Jeep Wrangler JK final year engine

years.preview: JL Generation Launch: New Platform, New Engines
years.description: The JL brought a lighter chassis, improved refinement, and new engine options including the 2.0L turbo and eTorque mild-hybrid.
keyChanges: All-new JL platform with aluminium body panels
2.0L GME T4 turbo petrol and 3.6L ERC Pentastar with eTorque introduced
3.0L EcoDiesel announced for later JL models
mainEngines: ENS - 2.8L CRD diesel (carryover for early JL diesel)
knownFor: 2.0T offers Pentastar power with better fuel economy
eTorque mild-hybrid adds complexity but improves stop-start smoothness
Early JL diesel still uses 2.8 CRD before 3.0 EcoDiesel arrival
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2019 Jeep Wrangler replacement engines

years.description: The 3.0L EcoDiesel and flagship Rubicon 392 HEMI expanded the JL's appeal, while Pentastar updates addressed earlier reliability concerns.
keyChanges: 3.0L VM L630 EcoDiesel V6 introduced for JL diesel models
Rubicon 392 launched with 6.4L HEMI V8 (performance flagship)
Pentastar rocker arm design refined on latest ERC units
knownFor: EcoDiesel offers refinement but timing chain vigilance required on early units
Rubicon 392 HEMI delivers 470hp but specialist rebuild costs apply
Latest Pentastar units benefit from updated rocker arm design
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-present Jeep Wrangler replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Wrangler 3.0 EcoDiesel timing chain • Rubicon 392 HEMI engine cost • JL Pentastar eTorque replacement

faq.h2: Jeep Wrangler Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Jeep Wrangler engine in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine costs range from £1,295 for a 2.8 CRD diesel to £4,500 for a 3.6 Pentastar, with supply & fit labour adding £800-£1,800 depending on workshop location.
keyPoints: 2.8 CRD diesel rebuilt: £1,295-£4,200 supply only
3.6 Pentastar rebuilt: £1,495-£4,500 supply only
Labour for supply & fit: £800-£1,800 additional
Used engines start around £950 but carry higher risk
All rebuilt units include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler engine replacement prices for your exact model

items.question: What is the most common engine problem on Jeep Wranglers?
items.answer: The 3.6L Pentastar's rocker arm failure and the 2.8 CRD's injector seal "Black Death" are the two most frequently reported catastrophic failures driving replacement enquiries in the UK.
keyPoints: Pentastar rocker arms wear from 60k miles, damaging camshaft
2.8 CRD injector seals fail, causing soot contamination
Cylinder head cracks affect 2011-2013 Pentastar castings
4.0 TJ heads (0331 casting) crack between valves 3 & 4
Early warning signs include ticking noises or black soot around injectors
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Jeep Wrangler engines with upgraded failure-prone components

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Jeep Wrangler?
items.answer: Yes - Wranglers hold exceptional residual value, so a £3,000 engine replacement often extends the life of a £15,000 JK or JL by another 100,000 miles, making it financially sensible versus part-exchange.
keyPoints: Wranglers depreciate slower than most SUVs
Rebuilt engine cost typically 15-25% of vehicle value
Off-road capability and enthusiast demand support strong resale
Upgraded rebuilt units address known failure points permanently
Supply & fit options available nationwide for non-mechanics
items.cta: Calculate if engine replacement makes sense for your Jeep Wrangler's value

items.question: How long does a Jeep Wrangler engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Well-maintained 2.8 CRD diesels can reach 180,000 miles, while 3.6 Pentastar petrol engines typically last 150,000-200,000 miles if rocker arms and oil changes are monitored closely.
keyPoints: 2.8 CRD: 150k-180k miles with timely injector seal checks
3.6 Pentastar: 150k-200k miles with rocker arm vigilance
4.0 AMC straight-six: 200k+ miles if 0331 head crack avoided
Oil changes every 5k-7k miles critical for all engines
Off-road use increases wear; inspect after heavy trail use
items.cta: Find a rebuilt Jeep Wrangler engine with upgraded longevity components

items.question: Can I swap a different engine into my Jeep Wrangler?
items.answer: Yes - Wranglers are popular for HEMI V8 or Cummins diesel swaps, but these require specialist fabrication, ECU tuning, and significantly higher budgets than OEM-spec rebuilt replacements.
keyPoints: HEMI 5.7L/6.4L swaps popular but cost £8k-£15k+ fully fitted
Cummins 2.8R/3.0T diesel swaps require custom mounts and wiring
OEM-spec rebuilt engines offer plug-and-play compatibility
Swaps may affect insurance, MOT and resale value
Always use a Jeep-specialist workshop for complex conversions
items.cta: Compare OEM-spec rebuilt Jeep Wrangler engine prices before considering a swap

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned and used Jeep Wrangler engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt/reconditioned engines are stripped, inspected and have worn parts replaced with upgraded components; used engines are low-mileage donor units sold as-is with shorter warranties and higher uncertainty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: Full teardown, new bearings/rings/gaskets, upgraded failure points
Reconditioned: Similar to rebuilt; terminology varies by supplier
Used: Donor engine from accident vehicle; compression test essential
Rebuilt units include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty typically
Used engines cheaper upfront but carry hidden wear risk
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Jeep Wrangler engine options with warranty details

items.question: Will a JL Wrangler engine fit a JK model?
items.answer: No - while both use the 3.6L Pentastar, JL engines have different mounts, wiring harnesses, cooling systems and ECU protocols; direct swaps require extensive modification and are not recommended.
keyPoints: Pentastar block architecture similar but ancillaries differ
JL eTorque mild-hybrid components incompatible with JK wiring
Mounting points, transmission bellhousing and ECU calibration vary
Source a JK-spec engine for JK models to ensure plug-and-play fitment
JL engines only suitable for JL chassis without major fabrication
items.cta: Get quotes for JK-spec or JL-spec Jeep Wrangler engines to match your chassis

items.question: Do you offer supply and fit service for Jeep Wrangler engine replacements?
items.answer: Yes - select "supply & fit" at checkout to access our nationwide network of Jeep-specialist workshops, with fixed-price labour quotes provided upfront before you commit.
keyPoints: 100+ vetted UK workshops specialising in 4x4 and Jeep repairs
Fixed labour quotes provided before engine dispatch
Warranty covers both parts and fitting workmanship
Collection/delivery options available for non-drivable vehicles
Typical fitting time: 2-3 days depending on workshop schedule
items.cta: Find Jeep Wrangler engine supply & fit deals in your area

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Jeep Wrangler engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from the specialist rebuilder, with options to extend to 24 months on select units.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Upgraded components (rockers, seals, heads) included in coverage
Some suppliers offer 24-month extensions for premium rebuilt units
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wrangler rebuilt engine warranties from UK specialists

items.question: How quickly can I get a replacement engine for my Jeep Wrangler?
items.answer: Most rebuilt engines are dispatched within 1-3 working days from UK stock, with fitting typically completed in 2-3 days at a specialist workshop once the unit arrives.
keyPoints: UK stock of common codes (ENS, EKG, ERB, ERC) enables fast dispatch
Rare codes or performance units may require 5-10 day lead time
Supply-only engines shipped via pallet courier nationwide
Supply & fit bookings depend on workshop availability in your area
Enter your registration for instant stock and lead time confirmation
